From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Former trade union of the United Kingdom

The **United Rubber Workers of Great Britain** was an organisation representing workers involved in the processing of [rubber](/wiki/Rubber "Rubber") and other waterproof materials in the United Kingdom.

The union was founded in 1889 as the **Waterproof Trade Union**, then in 1891 changed its name to the **India Rubber, Cable and Asbestos Workers' Union**. Before [World War I](/wiki/World_War_I "World War I") it was renamed the **United Rubber Workers of Great Britain**. Its membership fell to only 312 in 1936, at the end of a long trade depression, but it survived and by 1945, membership had risen to more than 4,000 people. It then changed its name to the **Rubber, Plastic and Allied Workers' Union**.[[1]](#cite_note-marsh-1)

In 1974, the union merged into the [National Union of General and Municipal Workers](/wiki/National_Union_of_General_and_Municipal_Workers "National Union of General and Municipal Workers").[[1]](#cite_note-marsh-1)

## Secretaries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=United_Rubber_Workers_of_Great_Britain&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Secretaries")]

:   1910s: H. H. Duke
:   1936: [Herbert Eastwood](/wiki/Herbert_Eastwood "Herbert Eastwood")
:   1954: Laurence Walsh
